keys.name: apIdsToItemIds
keys.doc: AP item id (str) -> ER FullID granted on receipt.

keys.name: locationFlags
keys.doc: AP location id (str) -> its ER acquisition event flag; the flag-poll detection table.

keys.name: regionOpenFlags
keys.doc: '<Region> Lock' -> the region-open event flag set when that lock is received. Keys MUST be exactly '<Region> Lock' matching the client's COARSE_LOCK_ITEMS names.

keys.name: options
keys.doc: runtime option echo sub-dict; every client-read option lives here (features' top-level copies are legacy duplicates the client ignores).

subkeys.name: enable_dlc
subkeys.doc: RESOLVED DLC bool (dlc_only implies on); gates DLC map-reveal flags.

subkeys.name: completion_scaling
subkeys.doc: completion scaling on/off + curve id (nonzero = on; 4 = smoothstep).

subkeys.name: completion_scaling_floor
subkeys.doc: minimum scaling tier as percent of max, applied from the start.

subkeys.name: global_scadutree_blessing
subkeys.doc: DLC Scadutree blessing scope Choice value (0 off / 1 player_only / 2 scaled).

subkeys.name: auto_upgrade
subkeys.doc: auto-upgrade received weapons: 0 = off; nonzero = raise each received weapon to the player's highest held level on its smithing track (raise-only, cap +25 normal / +10 somber).

keys.name: regionSphereTargets
keys.doc: {str(i32 region id): i32 target}; flat per-region scaling targets. Keys must parse as i32 (region NAMES are silently dropped by the client -- the 2026-07 dark-scaling bug); ranges (regionSphereTargetRanges) are the live wire.

keys.name: regionSphereTargetRanges
keys.doc: [[lo,hi,target], ...] play_region/100 sub-id ranges -> scaling target; the live completion-scaling wire (SCALING_WIRE).

keys.name: dlcScadutreeFloorRanges
keys.doc: [[lo,hi,floor], ...] play_region/100 sub-id ranges -> Scadutree-blessing FLOOR level (0..20) per DLC region. Emitted ONLY when global_scadutree_blessing==2 and >=1 DLC region is kept. Client mode-2 writes max(held-fragment level, region floor) so DLC enemies' blessing assumption is met on arrival; the enemy 70xx sphere scaler is capped in these buckets to avoid double-counting.

keys.name: completionScalingBasis
keys.doc: scaling basis Choice VALUE (int 1 = sphere); client also tolerates the legacy string form ('sphere').

keys.name: areaLockFlags
keys.doc: [lo,hi,open_flag] play_region ranges; locked (kicked) while open_flag is unset. FOLDED 2026-07-06: no longer emitted -- the client derives these from regionOpenFlags + its static REGION_PLAY_IDS geometry (area_locks.py holds the mirror authority + a kept-region coverage assert; test_gf_data.py guards table drift). A legacy seed that still sends a non-empty areaLockFlags is honored by the client as-is.

keys.name: lockRevealFlags
keys.doc: '<Region> Lock' -> map-reveal/enforcement flags set on lock receipt. The client consumer is LIVE (region.rs:121); greenfield does not emit it yet.

keys.name: regionGraces
keys.doc: item_name -> grace warp flags lit when that item is RECEIVED. Usually keyed by '<Region> Lock' (bundle: all of the region's graces), but grace GATES also key a sub-area's graces on a KEY ITEM instead of the region Lock -- e.g. Raya Lucaria's graces key on 'Academy Glintstone Key' so they light on key receipt, not on the Liurnia Lock. Client MUST light on receipt of ANY keyed item, not just Locks.

keys.name: runeGatedGraces
keys.doc: str(N) -> grace warp flags lit only once the player has RECEIVED at least N Great Runes (any of greatRuneItemIds). Used for the Leyndell capital graces (folded into Altus) which vanilla gates behind 2 Great Runes; those graces are pulled from the Altus Lock bundle and moved here. Absent/empty when leyndell_runes_required = 0.

keys.name: greatRuneItemIds
keys.doc: FullIDs of every Great Rune item in this seed's pool -- the set the client counts RECEIVED items against to satisfy runeGatedGraces. Emitted only with runeGatedGraces.

keys.name: startRegion
keys.doc: name of the always-kept start region (diagnostic + start anchor).

keys.name: startGraces
keys.doc: grace flags lit at spawn so the first warp is possible (front-door of start region).

keys.name: startItems
keys.doc: FullIDs granted once at game start (Torch, Spectral Steed Whistle, ...).

keys.name: reveal_all_maps
keys.doc: reveal the whole world map + underground view (client owns the RE'd flag set).

keys.name: progressionSurfaceLocations
keys.doc: AP location ids on THIS seed's progression surface -- the ONLY locations that may hold this world's own progression (region Locks, required/gate Great Runes, folded legacy keys). Enia (EniaShop) always excluded. The client stars exactly these, so 'where the locks can be' and 'what the tracker points at' are ONE set. REPLACES bigTicketLocations, which named a set progression could never reach (MajorBoss and GreatRune are not on the surface).

keys.name: goalLocations
keys.doc: AP location ids whose completion == victory; client sends Goal when all are done.

keys.name: checkItemFlags
keys.doc: vanilla FullID (str) -> the check flags it belongs to; suppresses the vanilla bag-add.

keys.name: shopRowFlags
keys.doc: ShopLineupParam row id -> eventFlag_forStock written for shop checks.

keys.name: checkLotBlankMap
keys.doc: ItemLotParam_MAP lot id -> the GOODS slot indices holding a check's vanilla ware. The client repoints those slots at apPlaceholderGoods, so the vanilla ware is never handed out at a check.

keys.name: checkLotBlankEnemy
keys.doc: Same, for ItemLotParam_ENEMY -- boss / enemy one-time drops. SEPARATE from the map table on purpose: the two tables can hold the SAME row id, so a merged dict loses the table and forces the client to guess. It guessed map-first, and every enemy lot that collided with a map id was therefore never blanked -- a boss that is 'just an enemy' handed out its vanilla drop and fired no check (playtest 2026-07-12, the Unsightly Catacombs duo, enemy lot 30120).

keys.name: progressiveGrants
keys.doc: item name -> ordered [{goods, flags, consumed}] granted on each successive receipt. `consumed` (REQUIRED bool): true = the player SPENDS these goods, so grant them exactly once via the ledger; false = the player KEEPS them, so self-heal them (unique_goods). A consumable shipped as kept is re-granted every time it is spent -- unbounded flask upgrades, then a CTD (playtest 2026-07-12).

keys.name: death_link
keys.doc: legacy top-level copy; the client reads options.death_link -- kept for back-compat.

keys.name: no_weapon_requirements
keys.doc: legacy top-level copy; the client reads options.no_weapon_requirements.

keys.name: enable_dlc
keys.doc: DLC / Land of Shadow regions active; the LIVE copy is options.enable_dlc.

keys.name: completion_scaling
keys.doc: legacy top-level copy of the scaling toggle/curve id (4 = smoothstep).

keys.name: completion_scaling_floor
keys.doc: legacy top-level copy of the scaling floor percent.

keys.name: global_scadutree_blessing
keys.doc: legacy top-level copy of the Scadutree blessing scope.

keys.name: versions
keys.doc: VERSION HANDSHAKE. 'apworld/<semver> contract/<hash8> data/<inputs_hash16>'. The client compares the contract hash to the one it was COMPILED against and shouts if they differ. Required, because the failure it catches is silent: apworld and client ship as two separate artifacts (apworld off-site, .dll on Nexus), so mixed versions are not an edge case, they are the norm -- and a stale .dll against a fresh apworld looks exactly like a bug in the game. Every report carries this string.

keys.name: world_logic
keys.doc: logic profile tag, e.g. 'region_lock'.

keys.name: region_count
keys.doc: len(kept) -- how many regions are in play this seed.

keys.name: ending_condition
keys.doc: resolved goal tag: 'region_locks' | 'great_runes'.

keys.name: great_runes_required
keys.doc: EFFECTIVE (clamped) Great Rune requirement for the great_runes ending.

keys.name: great_rune_items
keys.doc: required Great Rune item names this seed.

keys.name: bossLocations
keys.doc: {region: [boss AP location ids]} for kept regions.

keys.name: bossLockItems
keys.doc: {str(boss_flag): {name:'Felled: <Boss>', region, boss_ap_id [, gate:'Boss Key: <Boss>', display_key:<vanilla key name>]}}: kept BASE-game bosses always; DLC bosses added when boss_keys is ON. Client mints the 'Felled:' trophy on boss-defeat (mode A); the mode-B 'gate' (defer own check until key) and 'display_key' (legible vanilla lock name) ride the same entry when boss_keys is ON.

keys.name: filler_foreign_localized
keys.doc: count of distinct filler names forced local this seed.

keys.name: pool_builder
keys.doc: whether pool curation was enabled this seed.

keys.name: pool_builder_juice_added
keys.doc: resolved juice budget added by the pool builder.

keys.name: pool_builder_intensity_floor
keys.doc: resolved juice rarity floor (1..3).

keys.name: pool_builder_juice_candidates
keys.doc: size of the juice candidate set at this intensity.

keys.name: pool_builder_juice_pct
keys.doc: resolved share (0..100) of the Rune tail replaced with juice.

keys.name: locationIdsToKeys
keys.doc: matt slot key token per location; client resolves token1 -> flag (bedrock path).

keys.name: itemCounts
keys.doc: per-item quantity map {str(ap_item_id): qty}; client grants full_id x qty. Greenfield emits stack sizes for throwables (x10) and finished pots (x4) (features/filler_curation).

keys.name: naturalKeyTriggers
keys.doc: bedrock natural key triggers.

keys.name: lockGrantItems
keys.doc: items granted on a region lock receipt (bedrock).

keys.name: randomStartDoneFlag
keys.doc: bedrock random-start: flag set when the start warp completed.

keys.name: randomStartWarpFlag
keys.doc: bedrock random-start: flag that triggers the start warp.

keys.name: randomStartAreaId
keys.doc: bedrock random-start: destination area id.

keys.name: randomStartGraceId
keys.doc: bedrock random-start: destination grace id.

keys.name: fogWalls
keys.doc: bedrock fog-wall spec.

keys.name: fogWallDebug
keys.doc: bedrock fog-wall debug toggle.
